overview: This insect is an African Moon Moth (Argema mimosae), a species in the giant silkworm moth family Saturniidae. Adult moths have no functional mouthparts and do not eat, relying instead on energy stored during their larval stage. Their distinct long tails are thought to confuse bats, which hunt them using sonar. They typically live for less than a week in their adult form. iNaturalist +3 4 sites African Moon Moth (Argema mimosae) · iNaturalist

This insect is an African Moon Moth (Argema mimosae), a species in the giant silkworm moth family Saturniidae.
Adult moths have no functional mouthparts and do not eat, relying instead on energy stored during their larval stage.
Their distinct long tails are thought to confuse bats, which hunt them using sonar.
They typically live for less than a week in their adult form.
